auto_reuse.description: Automatic-reuse eligibility. ABSENT means eligible (the default) — record this only when full-slide materialization/render QA has actually failed for this item. eligible:false keeps the item published and catalog/retrieval-browseable for human review, but bars it from AUTOMATIC scoring/selection; an explicit user selection is still fidelity-gated and fails closed at build.

build_scope.description: Buildability scope: is this a GENERIC template/component a short unrelated brief can safely scaffold and fill, or a SOURCE-SPECIFIC slide whose slots carry specific dates/names/context? Orthogonal to auto_reuse (QA failure) and immutable_text (baked artwork). CONSERVATIVE: ABSENT means unreviewed == NOT auto-buildable, so a high semantic score never auto-reuses an unreviewed or source-specific item. mode:generic is required to auto-reuse; mode:source-specific keeps the item published and catalog/manually-selectable but bars it from AUTOMATIC selection (an explicit user pick is still scaffold/fidelity-gated). Set from a real review of the item's slot contract (roles, count/capacity, source-context), not a filename or ID rule.

reason.description: Reviewer note grounded in the slot contract (e.g. '7 role-generic cover slots, fillable from any brief' or '23 interview-date/round slots specific to the source deck').

immutable_text.description: Outcome of the immutable-text audit (slide-system/scripts/audit_immutable_text.py): does this item's ARTWORK carry source-specific copy that no text slot can edit? The audit renders the item with EVERY slot empty — whatever words survive are immutable by definition. This cannot be decided by parsing the artifacts: the pipeline strips <text> out of visual.svg, so a real lockup survives only as OUTLINED VECTOR PATHS that no XML parse can see. The machine renders the evidence, a human classifies it, and this field records the verdict. ABSENT means not audited; absent keeps the item's existing behaviour (it is NOT treated as unresolved), so un-audited items are unaffected.

audit.description: clean = the empty-slot render shows no source-specific words (canonical brand marks and decorative glyphs do not count); every string a viewer reads comes from an editable slot, so automatic reuse is unrestricted. immutable = words survive that name a specific context; `contexts` are required and automatic reuse is allowed ONLY for a request that satisfies a COMPLETE context group. unresolved = audited but not safely classifiable (no render evidence, or the artifact changed since the audit); automatic reuse FAILS CLOSED until a human re-audits it.

items.description: One COMPLETE context: every term in this group must appear in the request.

contexts.description: REQUIRED when audit is `immutable`: the context(s) the fixed copy commits the item to, as groups of normalized terms. Matching is ALL-OF within a group and ANY-OF across groups, so a lockup naming a programme AND a year only unlocks for a request carrying both — a bare year is not that subject and fails closed. Several groups let one context be phrased more than one way (e.g. ['performance-review','2025'] or ['performance','review','2025']). Terms are compared through the scorer's shared canonicalization and also make the item MATCHABLE for decks about that context, since the baked copy is genuinely part of what the item says.

evidence.description: Fingerprint of the artifact inputs that determine what the empty-slot render shows, taken when the verdict was recorded. Content hashes only — never mtimes or machine paths — so the binding is reproducible on any checkout. An item with paths.visual is fingerprinted by its visual (plus its slot contract); one without carries its artwork in paths.preview and is fingerprinted by that, so the rule follows whatever actually decides the render. `build_registry.py` recomputes these when projecting the compact registry the scorer reads: if they no longer match (a re-extraction replaced visual.svg, the slot contract changed, or the artwork was redrawn), the verdict is stale evidence and the item is projected as `unresolved`, so automatic reuse fails closed until it is re-audited.

visual_sha256.description: sha256 of paths.visual — the background the empty-slot render shows.

slots_sha256.description: sha256 of paths.text_slots — which copy is editable, and therefore which surviving words are immutable. Absent when the item declares no slot contract.

preview_sha256.description: sha256 of paths.preview — the artwork of an item that declares no paths.visual (the raster brand assets). Absent when the item has a visual.

deps_sha256.description: sha256 aggregate over the local image files visual.svg references and materialization inlines into the background (sorted reference identity + content hash). Absent when the visual references no safe local image. build_registry recomputes it; a referenced tile.png changing invalidates the audit exactly like visual.svg changing.

reason.description: Human-readable audit finding: what the empty-slot render actually showed, and why it can or cannot be edited.
